mechanism: The collapsed Taylor coefficient (~1.0-1.2) both reflects and reinforces broken transmission. The Fed cannot raise rates aggressively because fiscal feedback creates self-defeating dynamics (higher rates increase debt service and deficit), and the inability to tighten sufficiently perpetuates the fiscal dominance regime.
regime_dependence: Active when debt service exceeds ~3% of GDP; intensifies non-linearly with debt/GDP. The relationship is self-reinforcing: broken transmission constrains the Taylor coefficient, which further breaks transmission.
